column1: # Effect-lifecycle transition corpus — SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH.
# This file is the conformance bridge between the Rust trace-conformance checker
# Both sides are pinned to it:
#            asserts check_trace ACCEPTS exactly these transitions and REJECTS every
#            kernel.maude permits each (and forbids it without its enabling fact).
# Only LEGAL transitions are listed; the Rust test treats every unlisted
# (from_status, event) pair as expected-REJECT (exhaustive negative coverage).
# Trace-level (folded) semantics: the models model a blocked effect returning to the
# ready set as blocked -> queued -> claimed; the observable trace folds the store's
# atomic re-check-and-unblock into a single claim, so the trace edge is blocked ->
# claimed. `whip retry` IS an observable event (effect.retried), so it is a first-class
# Events: claim start_run terminal_completed terminal_failed terminal_timed_out
# Statuses: queued blocked claimed running completed failed timed_out cancelled
